rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical cascade cyclization of olefinic amides with thiophenols/diselenides to synthesize sulfurated/selenylated benzoxazines, oxazolines, and iminoisobenzofurans without a metal catalyst or external oxidant
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of thiophenol generates a sulfur radical (which dimerizes reversibly to disulfide); cathodic reduction of the disulfide back to thiyl radical/thiolate closes the loop, with cathodic proton reduction to H2

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical INT-I (C-radical after thiyl addition to alkene, cyclized onto amide oxygen pending)
cyclized carbocation INT-II (after radical cyclization and anodic oxidation)

electron_flow: At the anode, thiophenol is oxidized by SET to a thiyl radical (RS•) which reversibly dimerizes to RS-SR; the disulfide is reduced at the cathode to a radical anion that fragments to RS• + RS−, regenerating thiyl radicals. RS• adds to the C=C of the olefinic amide to give alkyl radical INT-I, which undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the amide oxygen and a second anodic SET oxidation to cation INT-II, followed by deprotonation to the heterocyclic product; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO/radical trap control (radical pathway probe, short-time reaction)
probe_reagents.observation: Stopping reaction at 1.5 h gave product 3a in 10% yield and disulfide in 80% yield, supporting thiyl radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: Disulfide 2q as substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of disulfide 2q with N-(2-(prop-1-en-2-yl)phenyl)benzamide 1a gave 3a in 73% yield, confirming disulfide as a competent intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 4-chlorobenzenethiol 2e
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks: 1a at 1.80 V, 2e at 1.51 V — thiol is preferentially oxidized at the anode; reduction peak of diphenyl disulfide at −1.14 V indicates cathodic reduction of disulfide

notes: Mechanism is paired: anodic oxidation of thiol initiates the radical, while cathodic reduction of disulfide regenerates thiyl radical and produces H2. Authors also performed derivatizations (mCPBA oxidation to sulfoxide/sulfone, HCl hydrolysis of iminoisobenzofuran and oxazoline) which are downstream functionalizations, not mechanistic probes.
